urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P1GG-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS INDIAN FLAG REGISTRY WITH FIFTH VESSEL

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, saying the move will streamline services on key routes. The company noted the reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, reinforcing its long-term commitment to India.The reflagging changes the Vila do Conde's registration to the Indian flag and involves a 2,592 TEU capacity vessel.This effort fits a broader government push to grow an Indian-flag fleet, with Prime Minister Modi emphasizing self-sufficiency in shipping.CMA CGM has already reflagged five vessels since last year and plans a sixth by the end of May.Earlier this year, the company ordered six dual-fuel vessels from Cochin Shipyard for about $350 million and announced a planned $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. Executives are also exploring inland waterways and proposed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to strengthen maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMACGM'S GEMALINK EXPANSION SET TO DOUBLE VIETNAM PORT CAPACITY BY 2027

CMA CGM Group has announced Phase 2 of the Gemalink terminal expansion in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with the work beginning on 20th April 2026. The move reinforces CMA CGM's commitment to expanding port capacity in Southeast Asia and supporting robust, sustainable supply chains in the region. When finished, capacity will nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to around 3 million TEUs, with full operation expected by 2027. The plan adds 450 meters of quay in two segments, expands the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and introduces five additional Ship-to-Shore cranes, bringing the total to thirteen. Gemalink has quickly established itself as one of the region's most efficient terminals since its 2021 opening, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. Its location along key trade lanes makes it a strategic hub within CMACGM's network in Vung Tau. Christine Cabau Woehrel, CMA CGM Group Executive Vice President, said the expansion reflects a long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's strategy to become a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC OFFERING WITH DIRECT CORK CALLS TO NEW YORK AND SAVANNAH

CMA CGM Group has announced enhancements to its Transatlantic PAD service, adding Cork as a direct weekly link to New York and Savannah, effective 28th April 2026.The changes include deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations to improve schedule reliability and overall predictability for supply chains relying on these routes.The PAD service now features direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, a distinguishing feature not offered by other services, and connects with CMA CGM's Northern Europe network including Le Havre and Rotterdam to facilitate feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets.In the United States, the service benefits from CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals, providing priority berthing to help relieve port congestion and bolster service reliability.The PAD service is capable of handling pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, appealing to time sensitive and high value shipments and offering a competitive alternative to air freight. The Cork addition strengthens Ireland's access to major U.S. markets, complementing CMA CGM's two-decade presence in Ireland across three ports and up to six weekly sailings, linking the country to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting key sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RSGT-S02T-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM TAKES STRATEGIC STEP WITH FIFTH VESSEL REFLAGGING IN INDIA

MUMBAI: French container shipping line CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, as it looks to enhance its service efficiency for customers across key trade routes. This announcement comes on the 24th of April 2026, marking another significant milestone in CMACGM's ongoing investment and long-term commitment to the country.The reflagging process involves changing the country of registration (flag State) of a merchant ship. CMA CGM has positioned itself as a strong growth partner for India across maritime, logistics, and infrastructure sectors. The reflagging of the 2,592 TEUs capacity vessel, Vila do Conde, underscores this commitment and aligns with the Indian government's broader objectives to increase the number of vessels registered under the Indian flag.Reflagging of vessels is gaining traction in light of the government’s initiative to bolster a larger fleet owned by India. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of self-sufficiency in shipping last September, citing the ₹6 trillion expenditure to foreign shipping lines in maritime trade. Following this trend, CMACGM has successfully reflagged all five vessels since last year, with plans to flag its sixth vessel in the country by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy.CMA CGM has outlined several strategic initiatives to support India’s maritime ecosystem, recognizing the country’s increasingly significant role in global trade. In February, the company became the first major international carrier to order vessels from an Indian shipyard, signing a $350 million deal with state-owned Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels. Additionally, CMA CGM is planning a $300 million investment in logistics infrastructure in Maharashtra, as indicated by the company’s chairman Rodolphe Saade.CMACGM is also exploring opportunities in inland waterways while assessing viable business proposals. A senior executive from the company has advocated for the Indian government to leverage Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City) as a vessel registration hub. With the government focusing on registering vessels under the Indian flag, this initiative not only offers commercial benefits but is also seen as a step towards enhancing maritime security amidst global uncertainties.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS GEMALINK TERMINAL: A STRATEGIC MOVE FOR VIETNAM'S TRADE

CMA CGM Group has announced the launch of Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal, located in Cai Mep port in southern Vietnam. This significant project, which commenced on 20th April 2026, marks a crucial step in enhancing CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and reinforces its commitment to the region's economic development and sustainable supply chains.Upon completion, the terminal's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to approximately 3 million TEUs, with full operational capability anticipated by 2027. The expansion plan includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments and increasing the container yard's area from 32 to 44 hectares. Furthermore, the addition of five new Ship-to-Shore cranes will augment the current fleet from eight to thirteen, significantly boosting the terminal's operational efficiency.Gemalink terminal has quickly established itself as one of the most efficient terminals in the Vung Tau region since its opening in 2021, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. This rapid growth emphasizes the terminal's strategic importance within CMACGM's logistics network, as it is well-positioned along key maritime routes. Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, highlighted the expansion as a testament to the long-term partnership with Gemadept and a reflection of the shared vision for Vietnam's role in global supply chains.The expansion of Gemalink is a critical component of CMACGM's broader strategy to enhance its presence in Vietnam. The Group has maintained operations in Vietnam since 1989, currently employing over 550 people across five offices in major cities, including Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi. With 29 weekly services connecting seven ports in Vietnam, CMACGM aims to leverage its extensive intermodal network powered by CEVA Logistics to boost trade efficiency.This expansion is more than just an increase in capacity; it highlights CMA CGM's commitment to supporting Vietnam's vision of becoming a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050. As the global demand for freight services continues to rise, investments like the Gemalink terminal expansion will play a pivotal role in facilitating the movement of goods in the region.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC SERVICE, BOOSTING PORT OF CORK

The Port of Cork is set to enhance its role in international trade as CMA CGM Group, a leading global provider of sea, land, air, and logistics solutions, announces improvements to its Transatlantic PAD (Panama Direct) service. This enhancement, effective from 28th April 2026, marks a significant step in fortifying Ireland's connectivity to vital U.S. and rapidly growing Oceania markets.With the new service, Cork will enjoy a direct weekly connection to major U.S. ports in New York and Savannah, providing shippers with competitive transit times of approximately nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. This initiative is poised to enhance schedule reliability through the deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations, thereby improving overall predictability for supply chains reliant on these routes.CMACGM's enhanced PAD service distinguishes itself with unique market coverage, featuring direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available through other services. This new route also integrates seamlessly with CMACGM's extensive network across Northern Europe, including key ports like Le Havre and Rotterdam, thereby facilitating efficient feedering solutions to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the U.S., the service capitalizes on CMACGM's Port Liberty terminals, ensuring priority berthing and bolstering reliability amidst ongoing port congestion challenges.The versatility of the PAD service allows it to cater to a wide array of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo. This makes it particularly attractive for high-value and time-sensitive shipments, presenting a viable alternative to traditional air freight solutions. The addition of Cork to this route offers Irish exporters and importers a direct, reliable, and competitive means to access major U.S. markets, thus reducing transit times and enhancing supply chain predictability.CMA CGM has maintained a presence in Ireland for over two decades, enhancing the country's trade through regular maritime services and a dedicated local office. With operations at three Irish ports and up to six weekly sailings, the company is committed to ensuring robust connectivity and dependable service. As CMACGM continues to expand its global network, it connects Ireland to over 420 ports worldwide, supporting critical sectors of the Irish economy, including pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R47S-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:35ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM TAKES STRATEGIC STEP WITH FIFTH VESSEL REFLAGGING IN INDIA

MUMBAI: French container shipping line CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, as it looks to enhance its service efficiency for customers across key trade routes. This announcement comes on the 24th of April 2026, marking another significant milestone in CMACGM's ongoing investment and long-term commitment to the country.The reflagging process involves changing the country of registration (flag State) of a merchant ship. CMA CGM has positioned itself as a strong growth partner for India across maritime, logistics, and infrastructure sectors. The reflagging of the 2,592 TEUs capacity vessel, Vila do Conde, underscores this commitment and aligns with the Indian government's broader objectives to increase the number of vessels registered under the Indian flag.Reflagging of vessels is gaining traction in light of the government’s initiative to bolster a larger fleet owned by India. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of self-sufficiency in shipping last September, citing the ₹6 trillion expenditure to foreign shipping lines in maritime trade. Following this trend, CMACGM has successfully reflagged all five vessels since last year, with plans to flag its sixth vessel in the country by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy.CMA CGM has outlined several strategic initiatives to support India’s maritime ecosystem, recognizing the country’s increasingly significant role in global trade. In February, the company became the first major international carrier to order vessels from an Indian shipyard, signing a $350 million deal with state-owned Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels. Additionally, CMA CGM is planning a $300 million investment in logistics infrastructure in Maharashtra, as indicated by the company’s chairman Rodolphe Saade.CMACGM is also exploring opportunities in inland waterways while assessing viable business proposals. A senior executive from the company has advocated for the Indian government to leverage Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City) as a vessel registration hub. With the government focusing on registering vessels under the Indian flag, this initiative not only offers commercial benefits but is also seen as a step towards enhancing maritime security amidst global uncertainties.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS GEMALINK TERMINAL: A STRATEGIC MOVE FOR VIETNAM'S TRADE

CMA CGM Group has announced the launch of Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal, located in Cai Mep port in southern Vietnam. This significant project, which commenced on 20th April 2026, marks a crucial step in enhancing CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and reinforces its commitment to the region's economic development and sustainable supply chains.Upon completion, the terminal's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to approximately 3 million TEUs, with full operational capability anticipated by 2027. The expansion plan includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments and increasing the container yard's area from 32 to 44 hectares. Furthermore, the addition of five new Ship-to-Shore cranes will augment the current fleet from eight to thirteen, significantly boosting the terminal's operational efficiency.Gemalink terminal has quickly established itself as one of the most efficient terminals in the Vung Tau region since its opening in 2021, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. This rapid growth emphasizes the terminal's strategic importance within CMACGM's logistics network, as it is well-positioned along key maritime routes. Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, highlighted the expansion as a testament to the long-term partnership with Gemadept and a reflection of the shared vision for Vietnam's role in global supply chains.The expansion of Gemalink is a critical component of CMACGM's broader strategy to enhance its presence in Vietnam. The Group has maintained operations in Vietnam since 1989, currently employing over 550 people across five offices in major cities, including Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi. With 29 weekly services connecting seven ports in Vietnam, CMACGM aims to leverage its extensive intermodal network powered by CEVA Logistics to boost trade efficiency.This expansion is more than just an increase in capacity; it highlights CMA CGM's commitment to supporting Vietnam's vision of becoming a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050. As the global demand for freight services continues to rise, investments like the Gemalink terminal expansion will play a pivotal role in facilitating the movement of goods in the region.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC SERVICE, BOOSTING PORT OF CORK

The Port of Cork is set to enhance its role in international trade as CMA CGM Group, a leading global provider of sea, land, air, and logistics solutions, announces improvements to its Transatlantic PAD (Panama Direct) service. This enhancement, effective from 28th April 2026, marks a significant step in fortifying Ireland's connectivity to vital U.S. and rapidly growing Oceania markets.With the new service, Cork will enjoy a direct weekly connection to major U.S. ports in New York and Savannah, providing shippers with competitive transit times of approximately nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. This initiative is poised to enhance schedule reliability through the deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations, thereby improving overall predictability for supply chains reliant on these routes.CMACGM's enhanced PAD service distinguishes itself with unique market coverage, featuring direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available through other services. This new route also integrates seamlessly with CMACGM's extensive network across Northern Europe, including key ports like Le Havre and Rotterdam, thereby facilitating efficient feedering solutions to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the U.S., the service capitalizes on CMACGM's Port Liberty terminals, ensuring priority berthing and bolstering reliability amidst ongoing port congestion challenges.The versatility of the PAD service allows it to cater to a wide array of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo. This makes it particularly attractive for high-value and time-sensitive shipments, presenting a viable alternative to traditional air freight solutions. The addition of Cork to this route offers Irish exporters and importers a direct, reliable, and competitive means to access major U.S. markets, thus reducing transit times and enhancing supply chain predictability.CMA CGM has maintained a presence in Ireland for over two decades, enhancing the country's trade through regular maritime services and a dedicated local office. With operations at three Irish ports and up to six weekly sailings, the company is committed to ensuring robust connectivity and dependable service. As CMACGM continues to expand its global network, it connects Ireland to over 420 ports worldwide, supporting critical sectors of the Irish economy, including pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

value.Title: GOVERNOR DUNLEAVY TO WELCOME GLOBAL LEADERS TO ANCHORAGE FOR FIFTH ANNUAL ALASKA SUSTAINABLE ENERGY CONFERENCE

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-GSG3-S42T-T0K8-00000-00GOVERNOR DUNLEAVY TO WELCOME GLOBAL LEADERS TO ANCHORAGE FOR FIFTH ANNUAL ALASKA SUSTAINABLE ENERGY CONFERENCE2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisGOVERNOR DUNLEAVY TO WELCOME GLOBAL LEADERS TO ANCHORAGE FOR FIFTH ANNUAL ALASKA SUSTAINABLE ENERGY CONFERENCEStates News ServiceANCHORAGE, Alaska

The following information was released by the office of the Governor of Alaska:

Governor Mike Dunleavy will host energy leaders, policymakers, and industry experts from across the nation and around the world at the fifth annual Alaska Sustainable Energy Conference, taking place May 19-21, 2026, at the Dena'ina Center in Anchorage.

The three-day conference will bring together decision-makers, innovators, investors, researchers, and energy developers to explore Alaska's critical role in America's energy security and economic future, with a focus on liquefied natural gas and North Slope development, advanced nuclear power, renewable energy technologies, and carbon capture innovations.

"Alaska stands at the forefront of America's energy future," said Governor Dunleavy. "This conference showcases our state's vast energy potential and our commitment to responsibly developing Alaska's resources to power our communities, strengthen our economy, and enhance energy security for our nation and our allies in the Pacific."

The conference will feature high-profile keynote presentations, including U.S. Department of Energy Assistant Secretary Audrey Robertson and National Laboratory of the Rockies Director Jud Virden on Day 1. Governor Dunleavy will host a fireside chat with Glenfarne CEO and Founder Brendan Duval on the future of the Alaska LNG project, as well as a keynote lunch presentation featuring entrepreneur and author Magatte Wade.

Key sessions will cover Alaska's opportunities in sustainable aviation fuel production, the 50-year legacy of the Alaska Energy Authority, advanced nuclear power deployment including the Eielson Air Force Base pilot project, and carbon capture utilization and storage (CCUS) technology.

Energy industry leader Maynard Holt of Veriten will also host a special session featuring Repsol CEO Josu Jon Imaz.

Other notable speakers include NERC President Jim Robb, National Hydropower Association President Malcolm Woolf, and former U.S. Sen. Kyrsten Sinema.

Additional panels will explore Alaska's role in renewable energy development, forest management and carbon offsets, critical minerals, synthetic aviation fuels, and Louisiana's leadership in CCUS technology, offering lessons for Alaska's emerging carbon management programs. A special breakout track will also focus on energy issues and solutions for rural Alaska. A pre-conference welcome reception will also take place on May 18 at the Anchorage Museum.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S105-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ LIMITED U.S.-FLAGGED VESSELS TRANSIT HORMUZ AS CS ANTHEM COMPLETES PASSAGE

Crowley Maritime says its CS Anthem completed a transit of the Strait of Hormuz on the 5th of May, 2026, a move that offers visibility into the small set of U.S.-flagged ships departing the Persian Gulf since the crisis began.Crowley emphasized that crew safety and service continuity are top priorities and thanked U.S. Navy and government partners for their role in maritime safety, while not providing routing or security details.CS Anthem is one of two U.S.-flagged vessels to transit under Project Freedom, along with Alliance Fairfax of Maersk; both left the region under military escort.The transit underscores ongoing risk environment; MSP/TSP vessels remain part of crisis logistics, ensuring that critical U.S.-flagged capacity remains available for military support when needed.

+ PORT MCKENZIE STUDY SIGNALS CROWLEY MARITIME'S PUSH INTO ALASKA FUEL INFRASTRUCTURE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study a potential fuel-terminal development at Port McKenzie, a step in line with its Alaska expansion strategy. The January 7, 2026 announcement outlines a feasibility study for the facility, aimed at capitalizing on rising regional interest.The proposed terminal would help establish a reliable fuel supply chain for marine and land operations in Alaska, supporting local industries amid growing energy demand in the region.Strategically located in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough, the project could streamline Crowley's logistics while attracting other businesses and investors to Port McKenzie, strengthening the borough's economic landscape.Approval of the permit enables Crowley to advance studies and engage with stakeholders, potentially signaling a milestone in its Alaska growth plan and future Port McKenzie investments.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME CONSOLIDATES UNITS TO SCALE SHIPPING, LOGISTICS AND ENERGY SOLUTIONS

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler and Energy led by Kollin Fencil, to strengthen execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy arms.The Shipping and Logistics Division merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management into a single unit serving commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transport and warehousing.The Energy Division brings together Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions to drive innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging markets.Both Fowler and Fencil, who joined Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, have overseen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the reorganization will enable faster decision-making and greater adaptability to market shifts, helping Crowley maintain its leadership in maritime, energy and logistics.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ KENYA SET TO BENEFIT FROM CMA CGM'S INVESTMENT IN PORTS AND INLAND CORRIDORS TO BOLSTER REGIONAL TRADE

An agreement between CMA CGM and the Government of Kenya was signed to expand transport and logistics infrastructure in Kenya and the wider East and Central Africa region. The deal, finalized on 11th May 2026 at the Africa Forward Summit with President William Ruto and French President Emmanuel Macron present, targets greater port capacity, inland logistics, and freight-management systems across Kenya and the wider region.Macron announced a 700 million euro commitment to renovate two terminals at the Port of Mombasa as a centerpiece of the collaboration, part of CMA CGM’s broader effort to boost regional connectivity and supply-chain resilience.CMA CGM has operated in Kenya since 2005, linking Mombasa and Lamu to global markets and supporting inland transport corridors across East Africa. The company’s Africa strategy centres on expanding port and inland infrastructure, strengthening logistics integration, and supporting decarbonisation, with nine container terminals operated across the continent. In Kenya, the CMA CGM Foundation is also pursuing social projects such as expanding the I.O. Me001 Innovation Center in Mombasa and renovating the University of Nairobi football field.

+ CMA CGM FORMS HIGH-LEVEL COOPERATION TO BOOST EAST AFRICA SUPPLY CHAINS

CMA CGM Group has signed a high-level framework agreement with Kenyan authorities to advance Kenya's logistics and port capacity, announced at the Africa Forward Summit in the presence of the Presidents of the French and Kenyan Republics.The agreement aims to accelerate solutions that support the growth of supply chain flows in East and Central Africa, covering maritime demand, inland logistics, and freight management.Since establishing its presence in Kenya in 2005, CMA CGM has connected the ports of Mombasa and Lamu to global markets and helped develop inland corridors across East Africa.CMA CGM has operated in Africa for more than 50 years, pursuing a long-term development plan to secure port and inland capacities, create regional hubs, and integrate logistics solutions, while also pushing decarbonization of transport chains. The group now runs nine container terminals across Africa, including Kribi in Cameroon and Lekki in Nigeria, and its CMA CGM Foundation supports Kenya projects such as PharmaBoxes and Containers of Hope.

+ CMA CGM ANNOUNCES KENYA PACT TO BOOST PORT CAPACITY AND MULTIMODAL TRANSPORT

CMA CGM and the Government of Kenya signed a strategic partnership during the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi on 11th-12th May 2026 to support the development of transport and logistics infrastructure, including port capacity, inland logistics and freight management.The deal aims to explore joint investments in Kenya's logistics and port infrastructure to accommodate growing supply chain flows into and out of East and Central Africa and to strengthen the region's connections to global trade routes.CMA CGM has operated in Kenya since 2005, establishing crucial links between Mombasa and Lamu with global markets and inland corridors across East Africa. The new agreement places Kenya within CMA CGM's broader African network, which comprises nine container terminals on the continent.The group's long-term strategy in Africa focuses on securing port and inland capacity, developing regional hubs, integrating logistics services and decarbonizing transport chains. Ongoing and planned projects include the Kribi Container Terminal expansion in Cameroon, Lekki Deep Sea Port in Nigeria and plans for a deep-water terminal in Pointe-Noire, Republic ofCongo. The firm is also pursuing multimodal corridors through CEVA Logistics to improve reliability, transit times and overall logistics performance in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G30G-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES SAFE TRANSIT OF CS ANTHEM THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Crowley Maritime says its managed tanker CS Anthem has safely completed a transit of the Strait of Hormuz, a passage that has seen only a small number of U.S.-flagged ships depart the Persian Gulf since the latest crisis began. On the 5th of May, 2026, Crowley disclosed that the voyage through this critical chokepoint is complete.In its brief statement, Crowley stressed that crew safety and uninterrupted service for customers remain top priorities, and it credited U.S. Navy and government partners for maritime safety. It did not provide further operational details, noting a company policy against discussing vessel routing or security arrangements.CS Anthem is now one of two U.S.-flagged commercial vessels confirmed to have transited Hormuz under the Trump administration's 'Project Freedom' plan, which seeks to evacuate ships through Oman’s territorial waters in a secured zone. The other vessel, Maersk's Alliance Fairfax, also left the gulf under U.S. military escort, marking a notable step in Washington's efforts to guide stranded ships out of the region. Despite the transits, the overall Strait environment remains risky, with mines, drones, and a larger military presence dampening broader traffic. Industry observers say these moves are coordinated, one-off transits rather than broad reopenings. Both ships belong to MSP or TSP and reflect the role of U.S.-flagged tonnage in crisis logistics. CS Anthem, a 49,990-metric-ton medium-range tanker, is managed by Crowley under a bareboat charter.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME SECURES PERMIT TO STUDY FUEL TERMINAL AT PORT MCKENZIE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study the development of a large fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, a move that could broaden the company's footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough. The permit, granted for a comprehensive feasibility study, was announced on January 7, 2026, as part of Crowley's plan to explore a fuel-terminal complex serving both marine and land operations.If realized, the terminal would bolster the local fuel supply chain and support regional industries amid rising demand. By leveraging Port McKenzie's strategic location, Crowley aims to streamline operations and attract other businesses to the area, potentially marking a milestone for future investment and growth in the borough. Crowley now proceeds with stakeholder engagement and detailed feasibility work.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO ACCELERATE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation of its business structure, creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. The move is intended to boost efficiency, deliver greater value to customers and position the company for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division brings together Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management operations to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing. Fowler said the new structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain and drive growth through safe, efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted the reorganization will enable Crowley to innovate and scale more effectively in traditional and emerging energy markets, reinforcing the company's long-standing support of diverse energy needs across the United States. Since joining Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, Fowler and Fencil have helped improve financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will accelerate decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market demands, supporting Crowley's position as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM FORMS STRATEGIC KENYA PARTNERSHIP TO EXPAND PORT AND LOGISTICS CAPACITY

French shipping and logistics group CMACGM has signed a strategic partnership with the Government of Kenya to expand transport and logistics infrastructure. The agreement, finalized on 11th May 2026 at the Africa Forward Summit and witnessed by President William Ruto and French President Emmanuel Macron, aims to bolster port capacity, inland logistics, and freight management across Kenya and the wider East and Central African region.As part of the deal, Macron announced that CMACGM will invest 700 million euros to upgrade two terminals at the Port of Mombasa. The initiative sits within CMACGM's broader Africa strategy to improve connectivity between ports, inland corridors, and international markets, and to support decarbonisation of transport systems on the continent. CMACGM has operated in Kenya since 2005, linking Mombasa and Lamu to global markets and supporting inland transport routes across East Africa.The company highlighted social projects through the CMA CGM Foundation in Kenya, including expanding the I.O. Me001 Innovation Center in Mombasa and renovating the main football field at the University of Nairobi. CMACGM is headquartered in Marseille and operates in 177 countries with a fleet of over 700 vessels that transported more than 24 million containers globally in 2025.

+ CMACGMDEEPENS KENYA LOGISTICS TIES WITH GOVERNMENT FRAMEWORK

CMA CGM Group has signed a high-level framework agreement with Kenyan institutional authorities to advance Kenya's logistics and port capacity. The accord was announced during the Africa Forward Summit in the presence of the Presidents of the French and Kenyan Republics, M. Emmanuel Macron and M. William Ruto.The framework aims to accelerate solutions that support the growth of supply chain flows in East and Central Africa, including maritime demand, inland logistics, and freight management. Since establishing its presence in Kenya in 2005, CMACGM has connected the ports of Mombasa and Lamu to global markets and helped develop inland corridors across East Africa.The group operates and develops nine container terminals across Africa, including the Kribi Container Terminal in Cameroon and investments in the Lekki Deep Sea Port in Nigeria, underscoring CMACGM's role as a regional hub. Beyond infrastructure, CMACGM's foundation has announced two projects in Kenya to strengthen education and innovation, including PharmaBoxes mobile pharmacies and the Containers of Hope program delivering humanitarian aid worldwide.

+ CMACGM AND KENYA SIGN STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP TO BOLSTER EAST AFRICA LOGISTICS

During the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi on 11th-12th May 2026, CMA CGM signed a strategic partnership with the Government of Kenya to support the development of transport and logistics infrastructure, including port capacity, inland logistics and freight management. The agreement was reached in a ceremony attended by French President Emmanuel Macron and Kenyan President William Ruto.The collaboration aims to explore joint investments in Kenya's logistics and port infrastructure to support growing supply chain flows into and out of East and Central Africa and to strengthen the region's connections to global trade routes. CMA CGM said the partnership aligns with its long term strategy to secure port and inland capacity, develop regional hubs and decarbonize transport chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-X4N3-S254-Y4GP-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ SAUDI PORTS AUTHORITY LAUNCHES CMA CGM ROUTE TO STRENGTHEN TRADE LINKS

RIYADH: Mawani has added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port, expanding maritime connectivity to Asia and Europe. The service commenced on the 3rd of May 2026 and links Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.Mawani said the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 TEUs, supporting Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. The addition supports Mawani's ongoing push to boost the Kingdom's global logistics capabilities.The move aligns with the National Transport and Logistics Strategy, which aims to reinforce Saudi Arabia's standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.During this period Mawani has launched 18 maritime shipping services with a combined estimated capacity of 123,552 TEUs.Port activity figures show momentum, with March cargo throughput up 6.66 percent year on year and 667,882 TEUs handled in February, a 20.89 percent rise from a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains a critical gateway supported by advanced facilities to bolster international trade.

+ CMA CGM TRANSFERS TWO MORE SHIPS TO INDIA, LIFTS INDIAN-FLAG FLEET TO SIX

CMACGM has moved two more containerships into the Indian registry, expanding its Indian-flag fleet to six as the group solidifies its presence in the Indian market. The development follows earlier transfers of CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond and CMA CGM Manaus.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was re-flagged from Malta to India and has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023. Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the Semarang, built in 2007 with a deadweight of 38,000 and a 2,700 TEU capacity.These two new transfers bring CMA CGM to six Indian-flag vessels, a noteworthy step after it led the way among major carriers in moving ships to the Indian registry. Maersk and MSC have since followed suit, reflecting India’s tightened cabotage regime.India's new rules restrict foreign-flag vessels from carrying domestic cargo, and since January 2026 the Ports Ministry has rolled back a prior exemption for certain transshipment. CMA CGM has already started employing Indian seafarers on its Indian-flag vessels—about 1,000 now, with plans to reach 1,500 by year-end. Additionally, CMA CGM signed a February 2026 contract with Cochin Shipyard for LNG-powered newbuilds, underscoring its growth and sustainability push in the region.

+ INDIA EMERGES AS GROWTH ENGINE AS CMA CGM PUSHES AI AND ECOSYSTEM PARTNERSHIPS IN LOGISTICS

The logistics sector is undergoing a transformation as supply chains become more volatile and customer expectations rise. CMA CGM Group outlines India's emergence as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, driven by AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms that are reshaping port operations and customer experience. The move signals a shift from reactive to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM underscored its pledge to harness artificial intelligence to improve operational efficiency in logistics. The company is building AI-powered solutions to optimize supply chain management and curb disruptions caused by unpredictable market dynamics, leveraging analytics and machine learning to create a more resilient, responsive network for customers.CMACGM's plan involves forging partnerships with Indian tech companies to build a robust ecosystem that enables AI integration across logistics operations. This collaborative model not only accelerates innovation but also positions India as a pivotal player in the global logistics landscape. The firm envisions AI as a core element of its operating framework, driving smarter decision-making and enhanced customer service.The company is also investing in digital platforms that boost transparency and real-time shipment tracking. This capability helps businesses make informed decisions and improves overall performance, with the aim of delivering a seamless client experience and accelerating growth in e-commerce and manufacturing.With CMACGM leading the charge, the industry stands on the cusp of significant change. Its AI-first strategy highlights how ongoing innovation is essential to maintaining a competitive edge, and CMACGM's India-focused initiatives could serve as a model for others navigating modern, complex supply chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R4JW-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:35ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME SECURES PERMIT TO STUDY FUEL TERMINAL AT PORT MCKENZIE

Crowley Maritime has secured permission to study the development of a large fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, a move that could broaden the company's footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough. The permit, granted for a comprehensive feasibility study, was announced on January 7, 2026, as part of Crowley's plan to explore a fuel-terminal complex serving both marine and land operations.If realized, the terminal would bolster the local fuel supply chain and support regional industries amid rising demand. By leveraging Port McKenzie's strategic location, Crowley aims to streamline operations and attract other businesses to the area, potentially marking a milestone for future investment and growth in the borough. Crowley now proceeds with stakeholder engagement and detailed feasibility work.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO ACCELERATE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 a strategic consolidation of its business structure, creating two divisions: Shipping and Logistics led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. The move is intended to boost efficiency, deliver greater value to customers and position the company for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division brings together Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management operations to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing. Fowler said the new structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain and drive growth through safe, efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted the reorganization will enable Crowley to innovate and scale more effectively in traditional and emerging energy markets, reinforcing the company's long-standing support of diverse energy needs across the United States. Since joining Crowley as senior vice presidents in 2023, Fowler and Fencil have helped improve financial performance and operational efficiency. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will accelerate decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market demands, supporting Crowley's position as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR FORGE FAIRWATER HOLDINGS TO UNITE JONES ACT TANKER FLEET

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, via its Seabulk Tankers unit, have formed a joint venture named Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transport assets and to operate as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider. The agreement was finalized on January 2, 2026.Fairwater will combine the strengths of both companies to offer a range of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, along with third-party ship management services. The venture will oversee a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will also provide crewing and technical management for 21 additional vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will become Fairwater's CEO after the deal closes. Tom Crowley, chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, said the partnership aligns with Crowley's growth strategy and will strengthen its leadership in the U.S. maritime market. Operations are expected to begin after customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater set to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI EXPANDS MARITIME CONNECTIVITY WITH CMA CGM'S OCEAN RISE SERVICE

RIYADH: Maritime connectivity between Saudi Arabia and key Asian and European markets is expanding after Mawani added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port. The service began on 3rd of May 2026 and connects Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.Mawani said the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 TEUs and aims to support Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. The move aligns with the National Transport and Logistics Strategy to reinforce the Kingdom's standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.Mawani noted that this period has seen the launch of 18 maritime shipping services with a combined capacity of 123,552 TEUs, aimed at ensuring efficient delivery of goods and strengthening readiness across Saudi ports in collaboration with major carriers including MSC, Maersk, PIL, and Hapag-Lloyd. The Kingdom has also seen port activity grow, with March cargo throughput rising 6.66 percent year on year and 667,882 TEUs handled in February, up 20.89 percent from a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains a key gateway with advanced facilities to boost international trade.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS INDIAN REGISTRY WITH TWO ADDITIONAL VESSELS

CMACGM is transferring two more containerships into the Indian registry, bringing its Indian-flag fleet to six. The moves add to earlier transfers of CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond and CMA CGM Manaus, and come as the group responds to new cabotage rules in India.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was moved from the Maltese flag to India and has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023. Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the Semarang, built in 2007 with 38,000 DWT and a 2,700 TEU capacity.The moves underscore CMACGM's strategy to align with India's tightened cabotage regime, which bars foreign-flagged ships from carrying domestic cargo. Since January 2026, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways has rolled back an exemption that allowed some transshipment by foreign vessels. CMA CGM has already begun employing Indian seafarers on its registered ships, with around 1,000 currently and a target of 1,500 by year-end. The company also signed a February 2026 contract with Cochin Shipyard to build LNG-powered new vessels to support growth and sustainability in the region.

+ CMA CGM ADVANCES AI-FIRST STRATEGY TO REDEFINE INDIA'S LOGISTICS LANDSCAPE

As supply chains grow more volatile and customer expectations more exacting, logistics is undergoing a profound reinvention. CMA CGM Group explains how India is becoming a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, with AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms reshaping everything from port operations to customer service. The result is a shift from reactive logistics to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM highlighted its commitment to artificial intelligence to boost operational efficiencies in the logistics sector. It is pursuing AI-driven solutions to optimize supply chain management and reduce disruptions from volatile markets, using advanced analytics and machine learning to build a more resilient network that meets evolving customer demands.CMACGM's strategy centers on alliances with Indian tech firms to create an ecosystem that supports AI integration into logistics, while also investing in digital platforms that improve transparency and real-time shipment tracking. The company aims to embed AI as a core operational element, enabling smarter decision-making, better service, and stronger growth for e-commerce and manufacturing.
